name: Freedom Rise

verdict.text: Best starter card — no credit history needed, real Chase relationship

earn_rates.category: Dining — first 6 months, up to $6,000 spent (then 1.5%)

earn_note: The only starter/credit-builder card on the market with a flat 1.5% base — most competitors in this segment cap around 1%. The 3% dining intro rate is a limited-time offer for new cardholders' first 6 months, not a permanent category.

redemption_options.method: Statement credit / direct deposit / cash back

points.note: Technically Ultimate Rewards points, worth a flat 1¢ each with no minimum redemption threshold — cash back doesn't expire as long as the account stays open. No transfer value; this card is designed purely as a credit-building tool, not a points play.

transfer_partners.highlight: Not a transferable currency — Freedom Rise is a straightforward cash-back starter card.

credits.name: Autopay Enrollment Bonus
credits.subtitle: $25 one-time · first 3 months
credits.description: One-time $25 statement credit for enrolling in automatic payments within the first 3 months of account opening and staying enrolled for at least 90 days.
tips: This is a one-time new-cardholder bonus, not an annual credit — it won't recur in future years.
Autopay also protects your on-time payment history, which matters more for a starter card than almost anything else on it.

credits.name: DoorDash DashPass
credits.subtitle: 6 mo free, then $10/quarter promo
credits.description: 6 months of complimentary DashPass membership ($0 delivery fees on DoorDash/Caviar). Separately, up to $10/quarter in statement credits on non-restaurant (grocery/retail) DoorDash orders through Dec 2027.
tips: Cancel DashPass before the 6-month free trial ends if you don't want to start paying for it.
The $10/quarter non-restaurant credit is the only ongoing recurring value here.

insurance.detail: +1 year (on warranties ≤3 years, up to 4 years total)

protection_note: Thin, as expected for a starter card — the basics (purchase protection, extended warranty, modest trip cancellation) are there, but no rental car coverage and no cell phone protection.
rental_note: No rental car coverage at all — use a different card for rentals.

services.name: No prior credit history required
services.detail: Designed for first-time cardholders — no minimum credit score, though a Chase checking/savings account with $250+ can improve approval odds (not required).

services.name: Automatic credit limit review
services.detail: After 6 months of responsible use (on-time payments, manageable balances), Chase automatically reviews the account for a credit line increase.

services.name: Lyft bonus
services.detail: 2% total cash back on Lyft rides, bikes, and scooters through Sep 30, 2027 (an extra 0.5% on top of the 1.5% base).

services.name: Foreign transaction fee likely applies
services.detail: Chase doesn't list Freedom Rise among its no-foreign-transaction-fee cards; treat it as carrying the standard ~3% fee abroad and confirm before international use.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards
additional_cards.note: Chase doesn't market a distinct authorized-user program for Freedom Rise the way it does for its rewards cards — this is a single-cardholder credit-building product.

timeline.text: Freedom Rise becomes available for digital application — previously in-branch only.
Chase launches Freedom Rise as its first dedicated starter/credit-building card, requiring no prior credit history and initially available only in branches.
